overallConclusion: Across 8 companies, 47% of matched jobs appeared on the career page before Indeed, with an average career-page lead of 236 days. Career pages are consistently the primary source of truth for job postings.

companyResults.conclusion: For OpenAI, the career page partially precedes Glassdoor: 28% of matched jobs (24/87) appeared on the career page first, with an average lead of 118 days (median: 75 days). An additional 519 jobs (86% of career-page listings) were never indexed by Glassdoor at all. This confirms that the career page is the primary source of truth for job postings.

For Notion, the career page partially precedes Linkedin: 22% of matched jobs (10/45) appeared on the career page first, with an average lead of 119 days (median: 43 days). An additional 106 jobs (70% of career-page listings) were never indexed by Linkedin at all. This confirms that the career page is the primary source of truth for job postings.

For Deel, the career page partially precedes Linkedin: 0% of matched jobs (0/8) appeared on the career page first, with an average lead of 0 days (median: 0 days). An additional 232 jobs (97% of career-page listings) were never indexed by Linkedin at all. This confirms that the career page is the primary source of truth for job postings.

For Anthropic, the career page partially precedes Glassdoor: 0% of matched jobs (0/43) appeared on the career page first, with an average lead of 0 days (median: 0 days). An additional 344 jobs (89% of career-page listings) were never indexed by Glassdoor at all. This confirms that the career page is the primary source of truth for job postings.

For Figma, the career page strongly precedes Linkedin: 100% of matched jobs (21/21) appeared on the career page first, with an average lead of 532 days (median: 427 days). An additional 148 jobs (88% of career-page listings) were never indexed by Linkedin at all. This confirms that the career page is the primary source of truth for job postings.

For Zapier, the career page generally precedes Linkedin: 52% of matched jobs (30/58) appeared on the career page first, with an average lead of 146 days (median: 108 days). An additional 144 jobs (71% of career-page listings) were never indexed by Linkedin at all. This confirms that the career page is the primary source of truth for job postings.

For Intercom, the career page strongly precedes Linkedin: 83% of matched jobs (29/35) appeared on the career page first, with an average lead of 362 days (median: 379 days). An additional 130 jobs (79% of career-page listings) were never indexed by Linkedin at all. This confirms that the career page is the primary source of truth for job postings.

For Airtable, the career page strongly precedes Linkedin: 87% of matched jobs (20/23) appeared on the career page first, with an average lead of 612 days (median: 514 days). An additional 52 jobs (69% of career-page listings) were never indexed by Linkedin at all. This confirms that the career page is the primary source of truth for job postings.
